pattern: Minimum cost to reach each step depends only on the three steps below it, so a forward dynamic program with a fixed-width look-back solves it in linear time.
hints: Reaching step j is only possible from steps j minus one, two, or three, and each arrival adds the landing step's cost plus the squared jump distance.
Define the best cost to stand on each step and build it upward from the ground step whose cost is zero.
Let dp at step j be the minimum over reachable predecessors i of dp at i plus costs at j plus the jump distance squared; the answer is dp at step n.

edge_inputs.description: four steps taking a double jump at the end
four steps preferring two double jumps
cheap top step but distance penalty discourages long jumps
expensive middle steps push toward skipping

stress.description: long staircase with random step costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    costs = [rng.randrange(1, 10001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [n, costs]
